chunk:
CHUNK:016/016
This point is due to Albert Sacks and Richard Stewart. 1088
Vol. 85: 1060, 1976


The Lawyer as Friend
motivation his inclination and character suggest: idealism, greed,
curiosity, love of luxury, love of travel, a need for adventure or
repose; only so long as these lead him to give wise and faithful counsel. It is the task of the social system as a whole, and of all its citizens, to
work for the conditions under which everyone will benefit in fair
measure from the performance of doctors, lawyers, teachers, and
musicians. But I would not see the integrity of these roles undermined
in order that the millennium might come sooner. After all, it may
never come, and then what would we be left with? 1089
compressed_context:
CLAIM: POINT DUE TO ALBERT-SACKS ∧ RICHARD-STEWART [VOL:85:1060(1976)]
H2: THE-LAWYER-AS-FRIEND
MOTIVATION: INCLINATION ∧ CHARACTER SUGGEST: IDEALISM ∨ GREED ∨ CURIOSITY ;
  LUXURY-LOVE ∨ TRAVEL-LOVE ∨ ADVENTURE-NEED ∨ REPOSE-NEED
CONDITION: ABOVE-DRIVES → WISE ∧ FAITHFUL-COUNSEL
TASK: SOCIAL-SYSTEM ∧ CITIZENS → ENSURE CONDITIONS FOR FAIR-BENEFIT ;
  FROM DOCTORS ∧ LAWYERS ∧ TEACHERS ∧ MUSICIANS PERFORMANCE
CAUTION: ROLE-INTEGRITY ↛ COMPROMISE FOR EARLY-MILLENNIUM
NOTE: MILLENNIUM MAY NEVER-COME ∴ UNKNOWN-OUTCOME
